feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Clarification Needed on SAP User Locking and Deactivation Policy in S/4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
I would like to request clarification on the SAP user blocking/locking and deactivation process followed in current setup.

Could you please provide guidance on the following points:

  1. Automatic Blocking Due to Inactivity
    After how long of no login activity is a user automatically blocked/locked in SAP?
  2. Deactivation After Blocking
    Is there a subsequent deactivation step after a user is blocked?
    • If yes, does this follow IT policy?
    • Is any confirmation or approval required before deactivation?
  3. User Notifications
    Are users notified in advance before their account is blocked or deactivated?

This information will help us provide accurate support to users experiencing access issues.

entry.title: PR/PO costing duplicated to CC and WBS
entry.content:
The issue we are currently experiencing is Purchase Orders holding WBS elements and Cost Centers, yet Purchase Requisitions do not allow both real WBS elements and Cost Centers.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation Not Permitted for Vendor or Customer in Foreign Currency Valuation
entry.content:
When executing Foreign Currency Valuation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the system raises an error stating that Year-End or Mid-Year Valuation is not permitted for the selected valuation area when Vendor or Customer open items are included in the run.

The Year-End and Mid-Year indicators are visible in the job parameters. However, activating them together with Vendor or Customer valuation results in an error. The business requirement is to perform year-end foreign currency valuation for open items without an automatic reversal in the following fiscal period.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Activate scope item 4GR for 3SL conversion
entry.content:
Hello,

For now, our S4 hana cloud public solution runs on a 2SL lanscape, and we will soon move to a 3SL solution. Therefore, SAP asks for scope item 4GR to be activated first.
Could someone explain the reason why this scope item is necessary to fulfill the conversion ? Is 4GR more than a "simple" business process ? Are there some technical blocks deployed when this scope item is activated that comply with à 3SL architecture and ensure a successful migration?

Thank

entry.title: Sales Kits processing using third-party item categories
entry.content:
Good day! I'm requesting for assistance regarding Sales Order for Sales Kits 31Q.

Based on the scope item process, it appears to be limited to standard sales order processing. May I ask if it is also possible to create a sales order where a sales kit can generate a purchase requisition?

During our testing, we observed that third-party item categories are not available when using sales kits upon sales order creation. We would appreciate your guidance on whether this scenario is supported and, if so, what configuration may be required.

Thank you in advance for your assistance.

entry.title: SAP to Microsoft Excel Integration for trial balance
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

We are trying to Integrate between SAP to Microsoft Excel for trial balance report. For this we followed the below mention SAP blog, but we unable to get the trial balance API to integrate to Microsoft Excel. Can you please suggest the trial balance API and steps to integrate to Microsoft Excel.

S/4HANA Cloud Integration | Microsoft Excel - SAP Community

Regards

Sandeep

entry.title: 3ZB Billing Cost across affiliated companies document exchange is not working
entry.content:
Hello,

We are working on the 3ZB and have completed the set up following the instructions, unfortunately the documents are not being exchanged when we create the purchase order, we do not get the corresponding sales order created. Purchase order status stays as "Not yet sent" and we see no message in the logs. We followed the script for the 3ZB set up instruction as well.

entry.title: Requirement Date in Subcontracting Component SAP S4 HANA CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
Hello experts.

I have a question about why the system is using a date from 4 days ago as the Requirement Date in my Subcontracting Components?

I have understood and verified that my delivery date is calculated from my info record, as you can see.

 

 

 

 

But if we look at the subcontract component, the system indicates a Requirement Date  4 days ago, why is this happening?

In the master data of my component I do not have any planned delivery time I have deleted and created an info record for 17 days and the system still calculates 4 days ago.

 

But if I manually move the delivery date, the system automatically adjusts it by approximately 7 days.
I've read note 2176766 - Requirement date (re)calculation logic in subcontracting, but I don't understand the calculation logic.
I look forward to your comments.
Thank you very much.

entry.title: Foreign currency transactions must be recorded using the exchange rate valid at the invoice date
entry.content:
Context
We are using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for supplier invoices in foreign currency (company code in PERSON_NAME, CAD).

Business Requirement
According to Canadian accounting practices, foreign currency transactions must be recorded using the exchange rate valid at the invoice issue date (Invoice Date).

Question
In the Supplier Invoice Fiori app, the system always determines the exchange rate based on the Posting Date, and there is no visible or editable Translation / Conversion Date field.

Can SAP confirm:
1.    Whether it is possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to use the Invoice Date as the Translation Date for currency conversion?

Steps to Reproduce
1.    Open the Create Supplier Invoice app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
2.    Select a company code based in PERSON_NAME with company code currency = CAD.
3.    Create a supplier invoice in a foreign currency (e.g. USD).
4.    In the Basic Data section:
o    Enter an Invoice Date (e.g. 02/03/DIGITS).
o    Enter a Posting Date different from the Invoice Date (e.g. 02/07/DIGITS).
5.    Go to the Details tab.
6.    Observe that the system automatically proposes an Exchange Rate.
7.    Click on the Exchange Rate field and choose Get Exchange Rate.
8.    Observe that the exchange rate retrieved corresponds to the Posting Date, not to the Invoice Date.


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

Steps to Reproduce
1.    Open the Create Supplier Invoice app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.
2.    Select a company code based in PERSON_NAME with company code currency = CAD.
3.    Create a supplier invoice in a foreign currency (e.g. USD).
4.    In the Basic Data section:
o    Enter an Invoice Date (e.g. 02/03/DIGITS).
o    Enter a Posting Date different from the Invoice Date (e.g. 02/07/DIGITS).
5.    Go to the Details tab.
6.    Observe that the system automatically proposes an Exchange Rate.
7.    Click on the Exchange Rate field and choose Get Exchange Rate.
8.    Observe that the exchange rate retrieved corresponds to the Posting Date, not to the Invoice Date.




------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Custom Account Modifier (Z) Not Appearing in Automatic Account Determination – SAP S/4HANA GROW
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Grow) and facing an issue related to Automatic Account Determination.

Here is my scenario:

  • I created a custom movement type (copy of 201).

  • In the movement type configuration, I changed the Account Grouping (General Modification) from standard value VBR to a custom value Z03.

  • The movement type configuration was saved successfully.

However, when I go to:

SSCUI 100297 – Configure Automatic Postings
Transaction Key: GBB

I cannot find Z03 in the “General Modification” list.
Only standard values such as VBR, AUF, VBO, etc., are available.

My questions:

  1. Is it technically possible to use a custom Account Modifier (Z**) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

  2. Is there any way to expose custom account modifiers in Automatic Account Determination?

  3. Or is this restricted by SAP’s Cloud configuration framework?

I understand that Public Cloud follows clean core principles, but I would like to confirm whether this is a system limitation or if there is an additional configuration step required.

Any insights or best practice recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Custom General Modification (Z03) Not Available in GBB – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I am currently working in SAP S/4HANA Cloud (SAP Grow) and facing an issue related to Automatic Account Determination.

Here is my scenario:

I created a custom movement type (copy of 201).

In the movement type configuration, I changed the Account Grouping (General Modification) from standard value VBR to a custom value Z03.

The movement type configuration was saved successfully.

However, when I go to:

SSCUI 100297 – Configure Automatic Postings
Transaction Key: GBB

I cannot find Z03 in the “General Modification” list.
Only standard values such as VBR, AUF, VBO, etc., are available.

My questions:

Is it technically possible to use a custom Account Modifier (Z**) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Is there any way to expose custom account modifiers in Automatic Account Determination?

Or is this restricted by SAP’s Cloud configuration framework?

I understand that Public Cloud follows clean core principles, but I would like to confirm whether this is a system limitation or if there is an additional configuration step required.

Any insights or best practice recommendations would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud 2602 / Uninterupted Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
i have a question regarding the Configuration of the Workflow for Parked Invoice as Complete (WS00800303).
I have it setup as follows:
1. One Employee creates the Supplier invoice and parks it.
2. The second employee has to check the invoice and make changes if necessary.
3. The third employee makes a final check and posts the invoice

The issue for me is, that currently if the employee in step 2 or step 3 makes a change to the supplier invoice and parks it again, the workflow gets canceled and then restarts from the beginning. 
My question is, is there a way to configure the workflow in a way that the change to the invoice doesnt restart the complete workflow and it just goes to the next employee ? 

Best regards
Lukas

entry.title: Unable to add New EWM warehouse number to New Business partner in "Set warehouse Address"
entry.content:
I am unable to add the new EWM warehouse number to the new Business Partner in the “Set Warehouse Address” configuration.

I have created a new plant and a new EWM warehouse, and now I need to maintain the default party entities. However, when I try to add them, the system does not allow the entry and displays the error shown below.

Kindly assist in resolving this issue.

entry.title: Create Physical Inventory Documents
entry.content:
Are there any latest hot fixes or release notes for the app  - Create Physical Inventory Documents?

We are seeing too many filters and columns in app. This has confused user at many levels. For ex. Why are zero quantity batches displaying in app? Do we need to apply every time filter for current book quantity greater than zero? Earlier all those filters were not required to apply while creating physical inventory documents. Please let me know.

entry.title: Deficit of Stck w.vend.unr./rev X PC XXX IN PO Subcontracting SAP S/4HANA CLOUD PUBLIC EDITION
entry.content:
Good day experts.

I receive the following message when posting a Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document.

1. Create a subcontract order for 10 pieces.

2.Without performing the outbound delivery of the components, and going directly to Post Goods Receipt for Purchasing Document, the system allowed me to record an entry for 6 pieces, but it did not allow me to do so for the rest and showed me the message I attached.

3.Besides skipping outbound delivery, I also didn't have my components in stock.

Why does the system allow this? Is it a CBC setting? As you can see from the purchase order, the info record, and my material settings, I don't have it configured to allow this.

Thank you for your comments.

entry.title: Project Budget Report - Budget (AVC) Value
entry.content:
Hi!

We are building a Project Budget Report Custom Reporting App in Public Cloud.

Although there is a standard app which provides all the reporting details, but it is applicable only for services and not for materials.
To provide reporting details of materials + services, we have approached our client to build a custom app.

Currently the challenge we are facing is fetching the Budget (AVC) value.
I have checked CDS views related to WBS elements, but could not find any appropriate CDS where the Total budget for a WBS Element can be fetched.

As per the process, a project is created, a WBS is assigned under it and initial budget is uploaded.

The fields we are displaying in output is similar the standard app.

1. Actuals
2. Commitments
3. Withheld Commitments (displays values if document is under approval)
4. Available Budget
5. Total Budget

We have already implemented the logic to display the values in the 1st three fields based on the values of the documents posted (PR - PO - GR) and it is working fine

If we can fetch this total budget value, then subsequently we can apply the calculations to derive the Available Budget = (Total Budget - (Commitments+Witheld Commitments+Actuals) for a WBS Element. That way our report can be completed.

Can anyone help me the resolve this. We are at 80% completion for this development.

 

Thanks

entry.title: Product Division in SAP S4 HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi All,

Currently customer has 1 sales org. with 4 divisions. Products are segregated as per the division. Sales Org. (1000) having 4 different divisions (10, 20, 30, 40) for sales and 2 distribution channels (wholesale and Retail (newly added)). As per customer requirement, wholesale distribution channel cannot sell product across divisions in single sales order, but retail distribution channel must allow selling products across different divisions in a single sales order.

In Product Master if we maintain the division in basic data then same is extended to Sales org. as well applicable to all distribution channels. This will prevent selling products in Retail distribution channel when products from different divisions required in single sale order. If Division not maintained in the Product master, then system will not restrict selling products across the division in wholesale distribution channel. We have proposed creation of separate division (00-Common) used for Retail sales and maintenance of Product exclusion at sales area level. But customer is highlighting lot of data maintenance required for the exclusion. Any better suggestion will be helpful.
